_comment: PoE Incursion Temple room reference. Each room has a 3-tier chain (T1/T2/T3). T3 is the max upgrade.
_note: Priority: must_have = upgrade every time; high = upgrade when possible; medium = situational; low = generally skip

rooms.drops: Omnitect boss fight (T3). Drops Sacred Vessels (used for Vessel of Vinktar), ilvl 83 Incursion-influenced items, and Incursion-exclusive uniques (Apep's Slumber, Coward's Legacy, etc.).
rooms.notes: The boss room. Always the same layout slot. Sacred Vessels are required for Vessel of Vinktar crafting. Highest item level in the temple.

rooms.drops: Double-corrupts items placed on the altar. Possible outcomes: two implicits, bricks item, changes base type, or adds a vaal implicit. Only source of double-vaaled implicits outside of Vaal Temple.
rooms.notes: Top priority room. A double-corrupt can produce items with two powerful implicits worth hundreds of divines. Always upgrade to T3.

rooms.drops: Quality-modified gems (up to 23% quality), Vaal gems including rare Vaal skills, gem experience tomes. T3 improves both quantity and quality of gem drops.
rooms.notes: Best source of Vaal gem variants. Useful for farming Vaal skills (Vaal Summon Skeletons, Vaal Haste, etc.) and high-quality gem templates.

rooms.drops: Orbs of Unmaking (remove charged sockets), Vaal Orbs, Orbs of Scouring, and other socket-manipulation currency. T3 reliably drops Orbs of Unmaking.
rooms.notes: Orbs of Unmaking are valuable for removing linked sockets without bricking items. Good farming target for socket crafters.

rooms.drops: Currency chests containing a range of orbs (Chaos, Exalted, Regal, etc.). T3 significantly improves currency quantity and rarity.
rooms.notes: Consistent raw currency income. In a well-rolled temple, Palace of Wealth alone can cover the temple cost.

rooms.drops: Vaal Breachstones guaranteed at T3. Also drops Breach Splinters and Breachstones of all types.
rooms.notes: Only consistent source of Vaal Breachstones without farming Breach domains. Very valuable for Uber Breach farming.

rooms.drops: Map drops (T12–T16), Cartographer's Sextants, unique maps. T3 guarantees multiple high-tier map drops and extra sextants.
rooms.notes: Best room for sustaining high-tier atlas progression. Sextants add up over many temple runs.

rooms.drops: Multiple strongboxes including unique strongboxes and Cartographer's Strongboxes. T3 includes additional rare/unique strongboxes.
rooms.notes: Good for item farming runs. Cartographer's Strongboxes provide maps; unique strongboxes can drop high-value items. Less reliable than Palace of Wealth for raw currency.

rooms.drops: Weapons and armour pieces (body armours, helmets, shields) with Incursion influence (Citaqualotl mods for physical damage/armor). ilvl 80+ at T3.
rooms.notes: Good for farming physical damage weapon bases and armour bases with Incursion influence. Less universally useful than currency/crafting rooms.

rooms.drops: Rings, amulets, and belts with Incursion influence (Matatl mods for resistances/life). Good bases for crafting jewellery with elemental resistances.
rooms.notes: Useful when targeting life/resistance jewellery crafting bases. Less impactful compared to meta crafting rooms.

rooms.drops: Elixir of Immortality (instantly restores life, mana, and energy shield — works only inside the Temple). Also drops unique flasks.
rooms.notes: Temple-specific flask has no value outside the Temple. Unique flask drops can occasionally be valuable but not a reliable target.

rooms.drops: Tormented Spirits are released, possessing monsters. Possessed monsters drop extra items and currency. T3 has multiple high-power spirits.
rooms.notes: Excellent if you're clearing the temple efficiently. Tormented spirits significantly improve loot from monster kills. Synergises well with a currency-focused run.

rooms.drops: Items with fire damage or fire resistance modifiers. Drops weapons and armour with fire-related Incursion mods.
rooms.notes: Niche — only useful for fire-specific builds. Generally skip in favour of Corruption or currency rooms.

rooms.drops: Items with lightning damage or lightning resistance mods. Drops weapons and armour with lightning-related Incursion mods.
rooms.notes: Niche — only useful for lightning-specific builds. Generally skip.

rooms.drops: Items with cold damage or cold resistance mods. Drops weapons and armour with cold-related Incursion mods.
rooms.notes: Niche — only useful for cold-specific builds. Generally skip.

rooms.drops: Adds defensive mechanisms to the temple (traps, turrets). Slightly more monster density from triggered traps. Drops armour-focused gear.
rooms.notes: Minimal loot value. Not worth upgrading unless no better option is available in that tile.

rooms.drops: Chaos-damage and ailment-related items with Tacati mods (chaos damage, poison chance). Can drop Concoctions (hybrid life/mana flasks).
rooms.notes: Only relevant for chaos or poison builds looking for specific bases. Otherwise low priority.

rooms.drops: Connects rooms and adds additional combat areas with basic monster drops. No unique drops at T3.
rooms.notes: Passage room — useful only for connecting isolated rooms. No direct loot value.

tips: Always upgrade the Apex of Ascension and Locus of Corruption — they are the highest-value rooms every run.
Architects in the same room compete: killing one locks the other out. Choose which chain to keep each encounter.
Two room slots of the same type can coexist on different floors — both can be upgraded independently.
The temple resets each league. You accumulate 11 Incursion encounters before the temple is complete.
Connected rooms are required to access the boss. Ensure a path to the Apex of Ascension exists.
